# EVRT Precision Residual-Force Null-Test Package v1.0

## Overview

This package provides a reproducibility-oriented experimental framework for evaluating candidate residual-force signatures in asymmetric nonequilibrium electromagnetic systems under controlled laboratory conditions.

The framework emphasizes:

- precision residual-force metrology
- uncertainty-aware diagnostics
- calibration discipline
- systematic artifact discrimination
- reproducibility-oriented methodology
- conservative scientific interpretation

The package is designed as a null-test-first experimental architecture. Failure to observe statistically significant residual-force signatures within the tested sensitivity range is considered scientifically meaningful because it constrains EVRT-like response amplitudes and improves metrological methodology.

## Package Objectives

- Establish calibrated torsion-balance workflows
- Characterize sensitivity limits and noise floors
- Perform controlled null-hypothesis stress testing
- Support reproducibility-oriented experimentation
- Standardize residual-force diagnostic procedures

## Repository Structure

- protocol/ — experimental workflow documentation
- apparatus/ — geometry, BOM, and setup information
- calibration/ — sensitivity and baseline calibration
- controls/ — artifact discrimination procedures
- data/ — raw and processed data structure
- analysis/ — scripts and uncertainty tools
- figures/ — diagnostic and apparatus figures
- results/ — reporting templates and summaries

## Scientific Position

The EVRT framework does not claim experimentally verified anomalous propulsion, antigravity, or violations of conservation laws. The package exists to support disciplined experimental investigation into candidate nonequilibrium electromagnetic response behavior under precision diagnostic conditions.
